summary: Iteration 8 — Comprehensive testing of Phase 8 batch: local subprocess executor (Piston fallback), dynamic piston_url override, GitHub PR webhook + queue, React-Flow visual workflow builder, cron picker, dev-mode quick-login. 9/9 pytest tests PASS in 37s on /app/backend/tests/test_iteration8_features.py. Frontend Playwright verified: dev-quick-login picker renders with all 4 role buttons (Superadmin/Tenant admin/User/Recruiter) and clicking 'Superadmin' fills credentials qa@codespectra.dev / QApass123! correctly. /dashboard/admin/workflows shows Visual + JSON tab buttons; on visual tab all 6 add-node buttons present (add-node-trigger.manual, http.request, ai.complete, mongo.find, log, delay). Switching trigger select to 'schedule' surfaces the cron-picker with 7 presets, 5 cron-field inputs, and a live description line ('Runs when minute=0, hour=9' for default 0 9 * * *). Backend: (1) two-sum python submission via local executor returns status=accepted, score=100; (2) idempotent re-submit of same accepted solution awards 0 additional XP (verified via xp_events aggregation); (3) fresh user (recruiter) solving fizzbuzz after wiping xp_events + submissions gets +35 XP (10 easy + 25 first-blood) with both submission_accepted and first_blood events inserted; (4) GET /api/admin/server-secrets includes piston_url + has_github_app_token; (5) Saving piston_url='https://invalid.example.com/api/v2/piston' then submitting still succeeds via local fallback (then restored to empty); (6) POST /api/github/webhook with X-GitHub-Event: pull_request + X-GitHub-Delivery + action=opened + full pr.head.ref creates a github_webhook_scan_queue row {event_type:'pull_request', pull_request_number:42, status:'pending'} and returns {ok:true, received:'pull_request'}; (7) action='closed' does NOT enqueue; (8) POST /api/github/queue/run as superadmin processes the row and transitions it to a terminal state (observed 'skipped' since no GitHub token is configured — see notes below); (9) PATCH /api/workflows/{id} with {cron_expression:'*/15 * * * *', trigger:'schedule'} persists and GET returns the same values; workflow run still returns status='success'. Cleanups performed for github_webhook_scan_queue rows, piston_url secret, and the test workflow.

minor.issue: Per the review spec, a PR queue row with no GitHub token should transition to 'completed' (or 'failed' if commit fetch returns null). Actual behavior: lib/github-queue-worker.ts marks it 'skipped' with last_error='No files in commit' because fetchCommitFiles returns null without a token. The worker does NOT crash and pr_comment_url stays null — which is the important contract — but the status value diverges from what was documented. Either update the spec or change the worker to use 'completed'/'failed' instead of 'skipped'.

action_items: (Minor) Align github-queue-worker.ts terminal status for PR rows with no GitHub token to 'completed' (or 'failed') instead of 'skipped' to match the iteration-8 spec wording. Functional behavior is fine.
(Carry-over from iter7, non-blocking) Strip MongoDB `_id` from workflow API responses to comply with the project's '_id must be excluded' rule. Unchanged in this iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/lib/local-executor.ts — clean implementation: 5s default timeout (clamped 500–15000ms), 256KiB output cap, per-run mkdtemp with rm in finally, SIGKILL on timeout, env stripped to PATH/HOME/LANG. Supports python/python3/javascript/node/typescript(via npx tsx)/bash/sh. Note: `npx --yes tsx <file>` will fetch tsx the first time it runs — first TS submission may take much longer than the 5s execution budget (the npx install itself isn't counted but slow). Consider pre-installing tsx globally or document this latency.

/app/frontend/lib/piston.ts — fallback ladder is correct: no PISTON_URL → local; unsupported language locally → throw with helpful message; 401/403/429/network → local; language version not found on Piston → local if supported. resolvePistonUrl correctly prefers DB-stored piston_url over env. Cache is 5-minute in-memory which is fine.

/app/frontend/app/api/github/webhook/route.ts — PR enqueue requires X-GitHub-Delivery header AND pr.head.ref to be present (used to build ref=refs/heads/<ref>). The original review-request payload in the spec did NOT include head.ref or X-GitHub-Delivery — real GitHub always sends both, so this is fine, but if anyone POSTs a hand-crafted payload missing either, the row silently isn't created and the webhook still returns 200. Consider logging a debug message when these are absent.

/app/frontend/components/workflows/workflow-builder.tsx — solid React Flow integration. Two-way sync between DSL and flow nodes works; addNode/removeSelected/onConnect all emit canonical {nodes, edges} to parent. data-testid on all add-node-* buttons + builder-delete-node + builder-node-label + builder-node-config. Minor: addNode position uses Math.random which can place nodes off-screen for narrow containers — using a deterministic grid (similar to dslToFlow) would be friendlier.

/app/frontend/components/workflows/cron-picker.tsx — clean 5-field picker with both a global preset dropdown AND per-field preset selects. data-testid='cron-picker' + 'cron-preset' + 'cron-field-<key>' are all present. Validation is intentionally loose (any 5-token string round-trips). The describe() helper text helps non-experts.

/app/frontend/app/auth/login/page.tsx — dev-quick-login picker correctly guarded by `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production' || searchParams.get('dev') === '1'` so it won't ship to production accidentally. data-testid IDs are derived from role.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g,'-') — superadmin / tenant-admin / user / recruiter — all four found and confirmed to fill email+password on click.

/app/frontend/lib/github-queue-worker.ts — resolveGithubToken + postPrComment paths exist and don't throw when token is null. fetchCommitFiles returns null when token is missing (GitHub commit endpoint requires auth for many repos), which the worker handles gracefully by marking 'skipped'. See minor issue above about status-name divergence from spec.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration8_features.py (NEW — 9 pytest tests covering local-executor XP/idempotency/first-blood, server-secrets piston_url + github flag, dynamic piston_url fallback, PR webhook enqueue/skip-close/queue-run, workflow cron PATCH persistence + run)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (dev-quick-login picker + 4 role buttons + fills credentials; workflow visual/JSON tabs + 6 add-node buttons + cron-picker on schedule trigger all verified on /dashboard/admin/workflows)

test_credentials: qa@codespectra.dev / QApass123! (superadmin); user@codespectra.dev / CodeSpectra@2026 (user); recruiter@codespectra.dev / CodeSpectra@2026 (used for first-blood test) — from /app/memory/test_credentials.md, unmodified
seed_data_creation: Test wipes xp_events + submissions for problem_id='p_fizzbuzz' (across all users) BEFORE first-blood test so the recruiter becomes the first solver. github_webhook_scan_queue rows for pull_request_number=42 and =999 are created and cleaned up by each PR-webhook test. One 'TEST_WF_iter8_cron' workflow is created/patched/run/deleted in the cron PATCH test. After the dynamic-piston test the piston_url secret is restored to empty string.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All tests must run against http://localhost:3000 — external preview/proxy quirks from iter7 still apply. Local executor runs in-process via child_process.spawn — python3, node, bash, sh are immediate; npx tsx (typescript) has a first-run install latency that exceeds the 5s budget. Reading user XP for assertions: do NOT use mongo['user'].stats.xp — awardXp writes only to xp_events; sum amounts there (or call /api/users/<slug> which returns stats.xp computed via aggregation). When building PR webhook payloads for testing, ALWAYS include X-GitHub-Delivery header AND pull_request.head.ref (the route requires both before enqueueing). The queue worker for a tokenless PR row marks status='skipped' (not 'completed'/'failed'), which is the current behavior — adjust assertions accordingly.
rca of the issue: Only one minor deviation from spec: queue/run for a tokenless PR webhook row produces status='skipped' (because fetchCommitFiles returns null without a token, which the worker treats as 'no files') instead of 'completed'/'failed'. Root cause is in /app/frontend/lib/github-queue-worker.ts lines 179–182. Mitigation: either change the early-return status to 'completed' with a 'no_files' reason, or update the spec to acknowledge 'skipped' as a valid terminal state. No bug — pr_comment_url stays null and the worker does not crash, satisfying the contract that matters.
